summary: Matched the Porygon2 item implementation by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itporygon2.s with src/melee/it/items/itporygon2.c and a new public header. The PR wires the C object into obj_files.mk, exports the Porygon2 state table and callbacks through itporygon2.h, and updates the central item logic table source to include that header instead of locally declaring Porygon2 symbols. No review comments or PR body were available, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added src/melee/it/items/itporygon2.c with C implementations for Porygon2 callbacks it_802D5ABC, it_802D5B14, it_802D5B60, it_802D5BBC, it_802D5BDC, it_802D5C00, it_802D5C60, it_802D5CB0, it_802D5CD0, and it_802D5CD8.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F8250[] with two state entries corresponding to the former assembly data table.
Added src/melee/it/items/itporygon2.h declaring the Porygon2 state table and callbacks.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/items/itporygon2.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itporygon2.s.o.

Updated src/melee/it/it_803F23CC.c to include <melee/it/items/itporygon2.h>, remove local declarations for it_802D5ABC, it_802D5CD8, and it_803F8250, and reference it_803F8250 directly in the Porygon2 ItemLogicTable entry.
Added prototypes to src/melee/it/it_266F.h for shared item helpers used by the new C file: it_80272C6C, it_80279CDC, it_8027A344, and it_8027A160.
smart_moves: Kept the matched function names at their address-based symbols, avoiding speculative renames while still moving the item from assembly to C.
Introduced a dedicated item header for Porygon2 and used it in the central item logic table, reducing ad-hoc extern declarations in it_803F23CC.c.
Represented the old flat assembly data table as a typed ItemStateTable array with two explicit initializer entries.
Used existing item access and state idioms such as GET_ITEM, Item_80268E5C, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, and Item_8026AE84 rather than open-coding structure offsets.
Captured hitlag effect behavior by assigning item->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and item->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll, matching the former stores to item offsets 0xD28 and 0xD2C.
Mapped the dynamic bone table access from assembly offset 0xBBC and bones[1] into item->xBBC_dynamicBoneTable->bones[1], making the matched behavior more readable.
decomp_lessons: For item assembly conversions, pair the new C file with a same-item header and include that header where the item logic table references the symbols.
When an assembly state table is a sequence of callback pointers and state IDs, convert it into ItemStateTable[] initializers rather than leaving raw extern data declarations.
Central item logic declarations should migrate from local void/extern declarations to proper headers once an item is matched.
Shared helper prototypes discovered during an item match belong in the broader item helper header when they are not item-specific.
Effect hitlag pause/resume fields can be identified from stores of efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll to item offsets and expressed with entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag fields.

Wrapper callbacks that simply call a shared helper and return false can be expressed directly; examples here include it_802D5BDC calling it_8026DA08 and returning false, and it_802D5CD0 returning false.
State transition callbacks can preserve animation switching behavior by using Item_80268E5C with state IDs 0 or 1 and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved address-based symbol names such as it_802D5ABC and it_803F8250 because no semantic names were evidenced.
The new header guard follows the project item-header pattern: MELEE_IT_ITPORYGON2_H.
The item source/header naming follows the item object name: src/melee/it/items/itporygon2.c and itporygon2.h.
The local item object variable is named item_gobj and the extracted item pointer is named item, matching common decomp readability style.
The dynamic bone table field retains its offset-derived name xBBC_dynamicBoneTable, indicating a known-but-not-fully-semantic struct field.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly object in TEXT_O_FILES with the C object to validate that the new C implementation matches the original codegen.
Matched assembly loads from item_gobj->0x2C by using GET_ITEM(item_gobj).
Matched calls to it_80279CDC with the item's scale field using it_80279CDC(item_gobj, item->scl).
Matched Item_8026AE84 calls with constants 0x273E and 0x273F, both using 0x7F and 0x40 as the remaining arguments.
Matched animation-state setup through Item_80268E5C(item_gobj, state_id, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE), with state 0 in it_802D5B14 and state 1 in it_802D5C00.
Matched conditional state behavior around it_80272C6C: one callback transitions by calling it_802D5C00 when false, while the other returns true when false.
Converted assembly access to the second dynamic bone into item->xBBC_dynamicBoneTable->bones[1] before calling it_8027A160.
Preserved simple forwarding callbacks to it_8027A344 and it_8026B894.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document it_802D5ABC through it_802D5CD8 for Porygon2 semantics.
Search for other Pokemon item matches that use efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll for hitlag callbacks.
Compare other item state table conversions from assembly data to ItemStateTable[] initializers.
Look for follow-up work on xBBC_dynamicBoneTable or bones[1] field naming.
Search for PRs that further clean up it_266F.h helper prototypes.
